channel.title: Changelog on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Changelog on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub renamed Copilot code review’s “Implement suggestion” button to “Fix with Copilot”. The updated flow now includes a UI dialog for more control when applying suggestions with the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent: Fast, cost-efficient models for simple tasks
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent now supports additional models, letting users choose fast, cost-efficient options for simpler tasks. The change expands the set of models available when delegating work to the cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask questions in context with Copilot on web
item.description:
Copilot chat on the web now opens directly on the page you’re viewing, so you can ask questions in context with less switching between pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub App installation tokens: Per-request override header
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a new GitHub App installation token format and is providing a per-request override header so developers can validate apps and workflows before the rollout completes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI agent and unified sessions view in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs now includes the Copilot CLI agent and a unified sessions view. The sessions view shows live status for running and queued sessions, and the update also adds an ask… feature.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot code review comments in the new pull requests experience to make them easier to scan and act on. The changes are available to all users opted into that experience and include grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated comment layout.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports creating new repositories directly in the app. On iOS, you can start from Home or Profile, tap the + button, and select Create repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now reports Copilot code review suggestions broken down by comment type. This adds a new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type metric for analyzing code review activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description:
GitHub now lets personal account owners set commit comments to be enabled or disabled by default for their repositories. This adds a user-level default for commit comment behavior across repos they own.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions concurrency groups now allow larger queues
item.description:
You can now allow multiple jobs or workflow runs to wait in the same GitHub Actions concurrency group instead of being limited to a single pending run. Previously, a concurrency…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Search and filter bar for repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added a search bar and filters to the Security tab’s repository security advisories list. This lets you search and narrow advisories directly within a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description:
GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the Copilot Student plan’s model picker starting today. The model is still available when using auto model selection.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Chat improvements for pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Chat has added richer context and new capabilities for working with diffs and pull requests. The feature is available when you ask questions about a pull request in Copilot Chat.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Global pull requests dashboard moves to opt-out public preview
item.description:
GitHub is moving the new global pull requests dashboard to an opt-out public preview, so it will be enabled by default for all users. The new dashboard and inbox experience is transitioning from opt-in to opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent fields added to usage metrics
item.description:
GitHub added a new used_copilot_cloud_agent boolean field to the Copilot usage metrics API in user-level reports. It mirrors the existing Copilot coding agent field after the rename to Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Disable commit comments across your organization
item.description:
GitHub now lets organization owners disable commit comments at the org level, removing the need to change each repository individually. This follows a prior repo-level option to disable commit comments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View and manage agent sessions from issues and projects
item.description:
GitHub now lets you view and steer cloud agent sessions directly from issues and projects. This adds visibility into agent activity without leaving the current workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new self-serve signups for GitHub Copilot Business for some organizations. The change is described as part of ongoing efforts to keep the Copilot experience reliable and sustainable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira: Our latest enhancements
item.description:
GitHub says it has added new enhancements to the GitHub Copilot cloud agent for Jira integration. The update focuses on making the integration more powerful and customizable, with more control for teams.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming change to Copilot usage metrics report download URLs
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot usage metrics report download links from Azure Front Door domains to a stable GitHub-owned custom domain. The update is intended to improve URL stability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
We have deprecated the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled GitHub Code Security configurations now…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
item.description:
GitHub is changing Copilot plans for individual users. The post says the changes are intended to support service reliability and sustainability, but the provided excerpt does not include the specific plan details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI supports custom registry based MCP allowlists
item.description:
Enterprise and organization administrators can now bring their own MCP registry and enforce allowlist policies in Copilot CLI. This feature is in public preview and brings MCP governance controls to…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Rule insights dashboard and unified filter bar
item.description:
GitHub announced a new rule insights dashboard and a unified filter bar for repository rulesets. The update is meant to make it easier to spot trends such as blocked-push spikes and bypass patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deployment context in repository properties and alerts
item.description:
GitHub added deployment context to repository properties and security alert pages. Two new built-in repository properties, deployable and deployed, now expose this information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates and product improvements
item.description:
GitHub says it is rolling out secret scanning updates that improve detection coverage, APIs, and related workflows. The post says these changes are part of ongoing secret scanning product improvements, but the provided excerpt does not include具体 details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub added a new “Fix with Copilot” button on github.com for resolving merge conflicts, powered by Copilot cloud agent. The feature lets users fix conflicts from a comment flow in three clicks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description:
GitHub is enforcing new usage limits for Copilot Pro+ and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from that plan. The change is intended to address high-concurrency and heavy-usage patterns.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description:
GitHub is pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro free trials because of increased abuse of the trial system. The change is intended to protect platform integrity for legitimate developers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description:
GitHub Actions workflow runs are now limited to 50 reruns per workflow. Attempts to rerun a workflow beyond that limit will fail with a failed check suite.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description:
GitHub moderators can now mark hidden comments as Low Quality from the Hide comment menu. The option is available on issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description:
GitHub added release tracking details to the issue sidebar, configurable default values for project fields, and faster navigation between related issues.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, March Releases
item.description:
VS Code moved to weekly stable releases, and this changelog covers Copilot-related updates in VS Code versions 1.111 through 1.115 shipped in March and early April 2026. The post says the highlights include Autopilot for fully automated workflows, but the provided excerpt does not include the detailed release items.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description:
GitHub has renamed the top-level Security tab to Security & quality across repositories, organizations, and enterprises on github.com. The navigation now groups code quality findings together with security-related information.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Faster, more flexible agent assignment from issues
item.description:
GitHub Mobile adds an Assign an Agent option in the issue overflow menu, making it faster to delegate work from an issue. The update is focused on quicker access to agent assignment and more flexible delegation from mobile.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent, formerly Copilot coding agent, is no longer limited to pull-request workflows. This expands how it can be used for research, planning, and coding tasks.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub secret scanning — coverage update
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning added nine new secret detectors from seven providers as part of a coverage update. The update includes detectors for providers such as Langchain, Salesforce, and Figma, along with additional secret types from Figma, Google, and OpenVSX.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3 Pro deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated Gemini 3 Pro across all GitHub Copilot experiences as of March 26, 2026. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements
item.description:
GitHub announced enhancements to the public preview of GitHub Copilot coding agent for Jira based on early customer feedback. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of security-related organization API fields
item.description:
On April 21, 2026, we’re deprecating and removing the following fields from the get an organization and update an organization REST API endpoints: advanced_security_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_alerts_enabled_for_new_repositories dependabot_security_updates_enabled_for_new_repositories dependency_graph_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_push_protection_enabled_for_new_repositories secret_scanning_validity_checks_enabled…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask @copilot to make changes to any pull request
item.description:
GitHub now lets you mention @copilot in any pull request to request changes directly in the PR. One announced use case is fixing failing GitHub Actions workflows and tests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now resolve auto model selection to actual models
item.description:
Copilot usage metrics now map activity that was previously shown as “Auto” to the actual model used when auto model selection is enabled. This gives clearer model-level reporting in Copilot usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: A smoother navigation experience in GitHub Mobile for Android
item.description:
GitHub Mobile for Android has updated its navigation to be more consistent and intuitive across the app. The change focuses on smoother movement between key areas like Home and other main sections.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More visibility into Copilot coding agent sessions
item.description:
GitHub added session log visibility for Copilot coding agent tasks. You can now review what the agent did while it worked in the background before approving its changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot coding agent now starts work 50% faster. The update applies when handing work to the agent through supported entry points such as assigning an issue to Copilot.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: View code and comments side-by-side in pull request Files changed page
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out docked panels on pull request “Files changed” pages so reviewers can keep pull request context open side-by-side while reviewing code. The panels include overview, comments, and merge status.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available
item.description:
Hierarchy view in GitHub Projects is now generally available. It is enabled by default for all new project views, and existing views can be updated to use it.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code Quality permissions removed from security manager role
item.description:
GitHub changed repository permissions for Code Quality: security managers can no longer enable or disable GitHub Code Quality unless they are also repository administrators. The ability to toggle Code Quality is now limited to repository admins.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality: Batch apply quality suggestions on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality findings on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for Code Quality findings in the Files changed tab by…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page
item.description:
We are deprecating the cost center integration on the enterprise People page and in the People/Licensing CSV export. When the cost center integration was originally shipped in 2023, cost center…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Optionally skip approval for Copilot coding agent Actions workflows
item.description:
When Copilot coding agent opens a pull request or pushes changes, Copilot is treated like an outside contributor in an open source project. GitHub Actions workflows do not run until…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Self-hosted runner minimum version enforcement paused
item.description:
Editor’s note (March 17, 2026): We updated this post to clarify that the v2.329.0 minimum version enforcement that has been paused applies only at configuration/registration time (i.e., when running ./config.sh…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot for students
item.description:
Starting today, students with GitHub Education benefits are now on the new GitHub Copilot Student plan. As part of this transition, we’ve updated the available model lineup. The new GitHub…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: REST API version 2026-03-10 is now available
item.description:
Previously, we introduced calendar-based versioning for our REST API, giving us a path to evolving our API while giving integrators plenty of time and clear guidance for upgrading. Now, we’re…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot auto model selection is generally available in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in JetBrains IDEs for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses a model on your behalf based on real-time model availability…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions OIDC tokens now support repository custom properties
item.description:
GitHub Actions OpenID Connect (OIDC) tokens now support repository custom properties as claims. Additionally, a new settings page is available in public preview, making it easy to configure OIDC token…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Issue fields: Structured issue metadata is in public preview
item.description:
Issue fields are now available in public preview for select GitHub organizations. If you’ve been using labels like priority/p0 or severity/high to track structured data in issues, you know the…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Request Copilot code review from GitHub CLI
item.description:
You can now request a review from GitHub Copilot directly from your terminal using the GitHub CLI. Whether you’re editing an existing pull request or creating a new one, Copilot…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Major agentic capabilities improvements in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
This update brings several new features and improvements to GitHub Copilot in JetBrains IDEs. Core agentic capabilities, including custom agents, sub-agents, and plan agent, are now generally available, with agent…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Explore a repository using Copilot on the web
item.description:
We’ve made it easier to explore a repository’s file structure when you chat with Copilot on the web. What’s changed Developers can now ask Copilot to show a file, then…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning pattern updates — March 2026
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning continually updates its detectors, validators, and analyzers. Here’s what’s new for March 2026. 28 new secret detectors from 15 providers, including Lark, Vercel, Snowflake, and Supabase. 39…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.24.3 adds Java 26 support and other improvements
item.description:
CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.24.3, which adds support for Java 26…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependabot now supports pre-commit hooks
item.description:
GitHub Dependabot now natively supports automatic dependency updates for pre-commit hooks. By adding pre-commit as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml configuration, Dependabot will parse your .pre-commit-config.yaml, check each hook’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Discover and manage agent activity with new session filters
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise AI Controls and the agent control plane add new session filters to help enterprises find and manage agent activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Pick a model for @copilot in pull request comments
item.description:
GitHub Copilot’s coding agent can be invoked in pull request comments via @copilot, and the feature adds the ability to choose which model @copilot uses for that request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — February update
item.description:
The February update to Visual Studio 2026 brings expanded agent capabilities, smarter code completions, and deeper Copilot integration across debugging, testing, and modernization workflows in Visual Studio. Highlights Here’s what’s…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality enterprise policy
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security enterprise policies now allow configuring GitHub Code Quality availability independently from Code Security.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes enterprise-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot enterprise usage metrics now include GitHub Copilot CLI telemetry, expanding what’s counted in enterprise-level reporting.

  • Source: GitHub
